platform.welcome.withHostname: Let’s set up {{host}} and tailor it to your needs.
platform.welcome.linuxGeneric: Let’s set up your Linux system and tailor it to your needs.
sidebar.runtimeStatus.title: Runtime & connection
sidebar.runtimeStatus.hint: Backup, restore and security are shown by the traffic lights on the dashboard—not here.
sidebar.closeWindowHint: Please close the window or tab manually (Ctrl+W).
risk.warning.security: Firewall, SSH and automatic updates change system configuration. Changes can usually be reverted.
risk.warning.controlCenter: Network, display and device settings are changed here. Only adjust if you understand the impact.
risk.warning.webserver: Installing and configuring web servers (Nginx/Apache) changes services and ports.
risk.warning.nas: NAS setup may adjust storage and services on the system.
risk.warning.devenv: The development environment installs extra software and may affect system paths.
risk.warning.raspberryPiConfig: Changes to Raspberry Pi configuration (config.txt, overclocking, boot options) can damage the system or make it unbootable. For experienced users only.
risk.warning.mailserver: A public mail server needs DNS, SPF, DKIM and a secure setup. Misconfiguration can cause open relays or blocklists. For experienced users only.
app.header.statusFallback: Open the dashboard for the traffic-light overview (backup, security, updates).
main.error.title: Failed to load
main.error.hint: Open developer tools (F12) → Console for details.
settings.language.label: Interface language
settings.language.hint: Applies to menus, buttons and help text in the app.
firstRun.step1.title: System check
firstRun.step1.intro: We briefly check which hardware SetupHelfer is running on so we can give better recommendations.
firstRun.companion.installSubtitle: The panda represents setup and modules; a green light means you can trust these first steps.
firstRun.storage.title: Storage & drive
firstRun.network.title: Internet connection
firstRun.network.online: Online – updates and app installs are possible.
firstRun.network.offline: Uncertain – if something fails to load, check your network connection.
firstRun.step2.experienceQuestion: To show you relevant content: how familiar are you with Linux and Raspberry Pi?
firstRun.experience.beginner.title: Beginner
firstRun.experience.beginner.desc: Clear explanations, safe defaults, only the most important features.
firstRun.experience.advanced.title: Advanced
firstRun.experience.advanced.desc: More settings and tools, still with risk hints.
firstRun.experience.developer.title: Developer
firstRun.experience.developer.desc: All modules visible, including dev environment, Docker, and server services.
firstRun.step3.title: Recommended first steps
firstRun.step3.intro: Choose where to start – or explore everything from the home screen.
firstRun.step4.title: What do you want to do?
firstRun.step4.intro: Pick one or more goals – we will suggest matching apps.
firstRun.step5.title: Recommended apps for you
firstRun.step5.intro: You can install these apps in the App Store with one click.
firstRun.reco.cloud.nextcloud.desc: Your own cloud for files & calendar
firstRun.firstStep.wizard.title: Set up system
firstRun.firstStep.wizard.desc: Guided assistant for basic configuration, security, and users.
firstRun.firstStep.appStore.title: Install apps
firstRun.firstStep.appStore.desc: Ready-made packages for media servers, NAS, smart home, and more.
firstRun.firstStep.backup.title: Create backup
firstRun.firstStep.backup.desc: Back up or restore before trying something new.
firstRun.firstStep.monitoring.title: Check system status
firstRun.firstStep.monitoring.desc: Keep an eye on CPU, memory, temperature, and services.
firstRun.firstStep.learning.title: Learn & explore
firstRun.firstStep.learning.desc: Examples and ideas for what you can do with your system.
firstRun.firstStep.controlCenter.title: Advanced features
firstRun.firstStep.controlCenter.desc: Network, services, and developer tools for advanced users.
dashboard.tooltip.sensor.vcgencmd:
What: Raspberry Pi SoC temperature (CPU/GPU).
Where: Onboard (vcgencmd).
Normal: about 40–70°C under load, >80°C critical – check cooling.
dashboard.tooltip.sensor.cpuPackage:
What: CPU package temperature.
Where: Onboard (thermal_zone).
Normal: often <90°C under load depending on vendor.
dashboard.tooltip.sensor.nvme:
What: NVMe SSD temperature.
Where: M.2 slot or PCIe.
Normal: typically 30–70°C; follow vendor specs.
dashboard.tooltip.sensor.gpu:
What: GPU temperature.
Where: PCIe or onboard.
Normal: often <85°C under load (vendor specs).
dashboard.tooltip.sensor.apu:
What: APU/iGPU or ACPI thermal zone.
Where: Onboard.
Normal: depends on vendor.
dashboard.tooltip.sensor.thermalZone:
What: Temperature sensor ({{name}}).
Where: /sys/class/thermal/{{zone}}.
Normal: follow vendor guidance (e.g. CPU <90°C).
dashboard.tooltip.sensor.generic:
What: {{name}}.
Where: {{zone}}.
Normal: follow vendor guidance.
dashboard.tooltip.disk.body:
What: Storage drive (HDD/SSD).
Where: {{where}}.
Normal: usage varies; >90% can hurt performance.
dashboard.tooltip.fan:
What: Fan (RPM).
Where: case/CPU cooler (hwmon).
Normal: speed depends on vendor and load; 0 RPM = fan off or not detected.
dashboard.tooltip.display:
What: Display output.
Where: {{output}}.
Normal: connected and resolution ({{mode}}) OK.
dashboard.toast.terminalOpened: Terminal opened – enter your password there.
dashboard.hero.title: Your {{device}} is running!
dashboard.hint.tempHigh: ⚠️ High temperature ({{temp}}°C) – check cooling. See Documentation → resource management.
dashboard.hint.lowRam: ℹ️ Less than 2 GB RAM – swap is recommended. See Settings or PI_OPTIMIZATION.md.
dashboard.help.appStore: In the App Store you will find Home Assistant, Nextcloud, Pi-hole and more – install with one click.
dashboard.systemStatus.loadingCard.title: Loading system traffic lights
dashboard.systemStatus.loadingCard.body: You’ll see the overall status and backup, restore, security, and updates shortly.
dashboard.systemStatus.bar.restore.yello: Restore: only dry-run confirmed – preview recommended.
dashboard.systemStatus.bar.allOk: No urgent notices – see the traffic lights below for details.
dashboard.systemStatus.bar.moreInCards: Other yellow items are shown in the traffic-light cards below.
dashboard.systemStatus.global.title: Overall status
dashboard.systemStatus.primary.restore.r: Sandbox restore has not completed successfully yet.
dashboard.systemStatus.primary.restore.y: Only a dry-run was confirmed – a preview restore is recommended.
dashboard.systemStatus.primary.backup.re: Backup has not been successfully verified yet.
dashboard.systemStatus.primary.backup.ye: Backup is only partially confirmed – please verify.
dashboard.systemStatus.primary.none: No urgent action needed. You can use the setup wizard if you like.
dashboard.firstSteps.title: First steps
dashboard.firstSteps.body: The recommendation in the box takes priority over the optional steps. You can hide this block.
dashboard.firstSteps.step1: At the top: overall status and four lights (left to right: backup, restore, security, updates). That shows where to act first.
dashboard.firstSteps.step2: Right below: your main next step in the highlighted box – use the button to open the matching screen.
dashboard.firstSteps.step3: Optional: run the “{{wizard}}” for basic configuration.
dashboard.firstSteps.step4: Optional: once the basics are in place, browse “{{apps}}” for add-ons.
dashboard.backendError.title: Could not reach server
dashboard.backendError.timeout: The server did not respond in time. Restart it or check the address.
dashboard.backendError.connection: Could not connect. The server may be down or the address is wrong.
dashboard.backendError.other: Overview, system updates and many settings only work when the server is reachable.
dashboard.backendError.solutionBody: Run ./start-backend.sh in the project folder. Or as a service: sudo systemctl start pi-installer-backend. If the server runs on another machine: Settings → General → set server URL (e.g. http://<IP>:8000).
diagnosis.local.step1: Check network and whether the device is reachable.
diagnosis.local.step2: Compare the server URL in settings with your setup guide.
diagnosis.local.step3: Start the backend or tap “Check connection” again later.
The web server likely failed to bind because the required TCP port (often 80 or 443) is already in use by another service.
In the overview, check whether nginx/apache is already running or another service blocks the ports.
Resolve the conflict (stop or reconfigure the other service) or move to a free port—often an advanced step.
Don’t re-run “Apply configuration” until the conflict is clear; use logs or documentation if unsure.
diagnosis.codes.backup_restore.verify_fa: Verification did not pass. That neither proves restore will work nor that it cannot—only that this check failed.
Do not start a restore until the cause is clear.
Pick another archive or create a new backup and verify again.
Check path and file permissions; use logs or support if unsure.
diagnosis.codes.system.backend_timeout.u: The connection to the SetupHelfer server hit a timeout. The device or network may be overloaded or unreachable.
diagnosis.codes.system.backend_connectio: No TCP connection to the SetupHelfer server. Often wrong URL, stopped service, or a firewall in between.
diagnosis.codes.system.backend_other.use: The connection failed. Check settings and whether the device is reachable.
diagnosis.codes.system.shared.actions.ch: Match the server URL in settings with your setup guide.
diagnosis.codes.system.shared.actions.re: Retry later; on a Pi check power and storage—or use logs/support.
diagnosis.codes.unknown.generic.title: No specific diagnosis
diagnosis.codes.unknown.generic.user_sum: There is no detailed classification for this message. Review technical details or contact support.
Expand the technical summary and review manually.
dashboard.updates.aptHint: Open a terminal and enter your password.
dashboard.network.title: Network – IP addresses
dashboard.network.reachableFromOthers: Reachable from other devices with this IP (e.g. http://{{ip}}:6680/iris for Mopidy Iris).
dashboard.sysinfo.title: System information
dashboard.chart.diskTipBody: If usage >90%, check performance; move large files to another partition/drive; use logrotate; clean temp files (e.g. /tmp) regularly.
dashboard.systemInfoCard.title: System information
dashboard.installStatus.title: Installation status
dashboard.installStatus.tooltip.security:
Set baseline security:
- Enable firewall (UFW) and review rules
- Enable Fail2Ban
- Harden SSH (e.g. disable password login)
- Automatic security updates
dashboard.installStatus.tooltip.users:
Add users:
- Admin vs standard
- Strong passwords
- Optional: SSH keys, groups/permissions
dashboard.installStatus.tooltip.dev:
Install dev tools:
- Python/Node
- C/C++ (gcc/g++)
- Java (JDK)
- Git, Docker (optional)
- Editor/IDE (VS Code Server/Cursor)
dashboard.installStatus.tooltip.web:
Set up hosting:
- Nginx or Apache
- PHP if needed
- Detect and map sites/apps
- Optional: CMS (WordPress/Nextcloud)
- Admin panels (Cockpit/Webmin)
dashboard.installStatus.tooltip.music: Mopidy, Volumio or Plex – playback when the service runs.
dashboard.cpuGpu.title: CPU & graphics
dashboard.sensors.title: Sensors & interfaces
dashboard.drivers.title: System drivers (kernel modules)
dashboard.modules.title: Modules overview
dashboard.modules.security.title: Security
dashboard.modules.security.tooltip:
Recommended first:
- Enable firewall (UFW) and maintain rules
- Fail2Ban for brute-force
- Harden SSH (ports/keys/password)
- Automatic security updates
dashboard.modules.users.title: Users
dashboard.modules.users.tooltip:
Multi-user setup:
- Create admin/standard users
- Strong passwords & groups
- Optional: SSH keys / sudo
- Clean up users
dashboard.modules.dev.title: Development
dashboard.modules.dev.tooltip:
For development on the Pi:
- Python & Node.js
- C/C++ (gcc/g++)
- Java (JDK)
- Git, Docker (optional)
- Editors/IDE (VS Code Server/Cursor)
- Version info and doc links
dashboard.modules.web.title: Web server
dashboard.modules.web.tooltip:
Web hosting on the Pi:
- Nginx/Apache + optional PHP
- Map sites/apps
- CMS (e.g. WordPress/Nextcloud)
- Admin panels (Cockpit/Webmin)
- Hardening & certificates
dashboard.modules.mail.title: Mail server
dashboard.modules.mail.tooltip:
Only if you really need mail on the Pi:
- Plan domain/DNS (MX/SPF/DKIM/DMARC)
- TLS/ports/firewall
- Spam/brute-force protection
- External relay
dashboard.modules.nas.title: NAS
dashboard.modules.nas.tooltip:
Pi as network storage:
- Samba (Windows/macOS)
- NFS (Linux)
- FTP/SFTP (external)
- Share permissions
- Optional: guest access & encryption
dashboard.modules.home.title: Home automation
dashboard.modules.home.tooltip:
Smart home on the Pi:
- Home Assistant / OpenHAB / Node-RED
- Ports & autostart
- Add-ons/integrations
- Remote access (securely!)
dashboard.modules.music.title: Music box
dashboard.modules.music.tooltip:
Pi as music/media station:
- Mopidy / Volumio / Plex
- AirPlay/Spotify (optional)
- Ports & autostart
- Web UI links
dashboard.modules.presets.title: Presets
dashboard.modules.presets.tooltip:
Quick setups via profile:
- NAS / web / home automation / music / learning PC
- Installs matching packages
- Sensible defaults
- Fewer mistakes
dashboard.modules.learning.title: Learning PC
dashboard.modules.learning.tooltip:
For school/projects:
- Scratch, Python/Jupyter
- GPIO/robotics libs
- Electronics tools
- Math/science tools + links
dashboard.modules.monitoring.title: Monitoring
dashboard.modules.monitoring.tooltip:
Monitoring:
- Node Exporter, Prometheus, Grafana
- Dashboards & health checks
- UI links
- Basis for alerts/logs
dashboard.modules.backup.title: Backup & restore
dashboard.modules.backup.tooltip:
Data safety:
- Create backups (full/incremental)
- List backups
- Restore (with warnings)
- Basis for reinstall without data loss
dashboard.quickstart.title: Quick start
dashboard.quickstart.body: Use the installation wizard to configure your system in a few steps.
dashboard.panda.introTitle: Your starting point for Linux and Raspberry Pi
dashboard.panda.introBody: The SetupHelfer panda guides you step by step: check, configure and understand your system.
dashboard.panda.companionStatusHint: The traffic light on the panda reflects overall status from backup, restore, security and updates (green = OK, yellow = review, red = act).
panda.role.helper: Visual helper for beginners – explains steps and highlights safe defaults.
panda.variant.backup.tip: Create a backup before making larger changes to your system.
panda.variant.security.tip: Start with firewall, SSH hardening and automatic security updates.
panda.variant.diagnostics.tip: If something goes wrong, the diagnostics view helps you spot typical issues.
backup.verify.basic.encryptedInfo: In basic mode, encrypted backups can only be checked for existence and size. For a deeper check, the backup must be decrypted.
backup.verify.basic.encryptedEmpty: ❌ Encrypted backup file is empty or not found.
backup.verify.deep.promptKey: Deep verification requires the encryption key. Please enter the password/key:
backup.verify.deep.title: Deep verification of encrypted backup
backup.verify.deep.body: Deep verification temporarily decrypts the backup and checks the archive contents. The decrypted file is removed again after verification.
backup.verify.deep.label: Password / key for the backup
backup.restore.onlyOne: Please select exactly one backup for analysis.
backup.restore.preview.confirm: Do you want to analyse backup {{file}} in test mode? The system will not be overwritten.
backup.restore.preview.sudoSubtitle: Elevated rights are required for the test restore. The backup is extracted into a preview directory without overwriting the system.
backup.restore.preview.toastSuccess: Test restore successful – the system was not overwritten.
backup.restore.incrementalNotSupported: Restore for incremental backups is currently not supported. Please use a full backup.
backup.preview.title: Analysis of test restore
backup.preview.info: The backup was extracted into a preview directory. The values below come from analysing the archive. The running system was not overwritten.
backup.preview.safeHint: Note: This test restore is non-destructive. Changes affect only the preview directory, not the running system.
backup.preview.systemLike.title: System-like paths in archive
backup.preview.systemLike.hint: These entries lie in typical system or user paths (e.g. /etc, /home). Carefully check whether a productive restore really makes sense.
backup.preview.blocked.title: Problematic archive entries detected
backup.preview.blocked.hint: The archive contains paths or file types that were blocked for safety reasons (e.g. path traversal, absolute paths, symlinks, device files). This backup should not be applied productively.
sudo.dialog.toast.invalidResponse: Can't reach the SetupHelfer service right now. Try again later or check Settings.
sudo.dialog.toast.serverNoPassword: Note: server reports no password. Multiple workers?
sudo.dialog.toast.saveFailedGeneric: Save didn’t work. Check the password or choose “Save without verification”.
sudo.dialog.toast.wrongPassword: That password wasn’t accepted. Try again or choose “Save without verification”.
sudo.dialog.toast.sudoTestTimeout: The check took too long. Try again or choose “Save without verification”.
sudo.dialog.toast.saveFailedServer: Couldn't save: the SetupHelfer service isn't reachable. Try again later or choose “Later”.
sudo.dialog.toast.skipHint: You can enter the sudo password later in user settings
sudo.dialog.title: Sudo password required
sudo.dialog.subtitle: Needed for installations and security settings.
sudo.dialog.serverUnreachable.title: Can't connect to the SetupHelfer service
sudo.dialog.serverUnreachable.body: Without it we can't verify or store your password safely right now. That's often normal—for example while the app is still starting or the service isn't running yet. You can still open the app with “Later” and read the help. Enter the password later under Settings once everything is connected.
sudo.dialog.serverUnreachable.technicalB: The backend must be reachable (usually port 8000). When developing from the project folder, start the backend script, restart the app, or check the server URL in Settings.
sudo.dialog.sessionHint: The sudo password is stored only for this session and not permanently.
sudo.dialog.passwordHelp: Used for: firewall, user management, system updates, etc.
sudo.dialog.skipTestLabel: Save without verification (default; verified on first use)
sudo.dialog.offlineExplain: Without a connection, saving a password here won’t work—choose “Later” or retry the check.
sudo.dialog.autoSkipOffline: The SetupHelfer service isn’t reachable right now. The app will open anyway—you can add the sudo password later under Settings.
sudo.modal.title: Sudo password required
sudo.modal.subtitle: Needed for installations and security settings.
sudo.modal.sessionHint: Input is hidden and stored only for this session.
sudo.modal.skipTestLabel: Save without verification (if the app hangs)
runningBackup.checkCloudHint: Checking within 1 min if file is in cloud…
backup.messages.error: An error occurred during the backup operation.
backup.messages.no_space: Not enough free disk space on the target.
backup.messages.mkdir_failed: The backup directory could not be created.
backup.messages.settings_retention_inval: Retention value must be between 1 and 100.
backup.messages.list_failed: Could not load the backup list.
backup.messages.job_conflict: Another backup or clone job is already running.
backup.messages.verify_archive_unreadabl: The archive could not be read.
backup.messages.verify_shallow_encrypted: Encrypted backup appears empty or invalid.
backup.messages.verify_module_load_faile: Backup module could not be loaded.
backup.messages.delete_missing_param: A valid backup file name is required.
backup.messages.restore_analyze_failed: The archive could not be analyzed.
backup.messages.restore_dry_run_ok: Dry run completed; nothing was written.
backup.messages.restore_preview_ok: Test restore completed; the live system was not overwritten.
backup.messages.restore_root_blocked: Full root restore is disabled in this version.
backup.messages.cloud_credentials_missin: Cloud credentials incomplete; upload skipped or failed.
backup.messages.cloud_upload_skipped: Saved locally only; cloud upload was skipped.
backup.messages.cloud_upload_file_missin: Local backup file missing for upload.
backup.messages.partial_success: Backup completed locally, but the cloud step did not finish successfully.
backup.messages.status_failed: Could not read backup tools status.
backup.messages.cloud_list_disabled: Cloud upload is disabled; no remote list.
backup.messages.cloud_list_s3_unsupporte: S3 listing is not supported yet.
backup.messages.cloud_list_provider_unsu: This provider is not supported for cloud listing.
backup.messages.cloud_list_credentials_m: WebDAV URL, username, or password is missing.
backup.messages.cloud_quota_disabled: Cloud upload is disabled; no quota.
backup.messages.cloud_quota_incomplete_s: Cloud settings are incomplete; no quota.
backup.messages.cloud_quota_provider_uns: Quota is not available for this provider.
backup.messages.cloud_delete_missing_par: A backup file or href is required.
backup.messages.cloud_delete_disabled: Cloud upload is disabled; delete is not possible.
backup.messages.cloud_delete_provider_un: Delete is not supported for this provider.
backup.messages.cloud_delete_invalid_res: Unexpected response while deleting from cloud.
backup.messages.cloud_delete_curl_failed: Network error while deleting from cloud.
backup.messages.clone_disk_info_failed: Could not load clone disk information.
backup.messages.clone_source_missing: Source device for clone not found.
backup.messages.clone_target_not_candida: Target is not a valid clone candidate.
backup.messages.clone_target_busy: Target is mounted and could not be unmounted.
backup.messages.clone_mountpoint_failed: Clone mount point could not be created.
backup.messages.clone_mount_failed: Clone target could not be mounted.
backup.messages.usb_prepare_mount_not_fo: Mount point or device not found.
backup.messages.usb_prepare_tool_missing: A required formatting tool is missing.
backup.messages.usb_still_mounted: USB is still in use; unmount failed.
backup.messages.usb_eject_busy: Cannot eject: device still mounted or busy.
backup.messages.job_warning: Note: the job finished with a warning (see log for details).
backup.messages.verify_samples_hidden: {{count}} sample files hidden from verification output.
backup.messages.verify_archive_invalid: The archive is invalid or corrupted.
backup.messages.delete_batch_partial: {{failed}} backup(s) could not be deleted. Affected (excerpt): {{files}}
backup.messages.cloud_delete_partial: {{failed}} cloud backup(s) could not be deleted. Affected (excerpt): {{files}}
backup.i18n.sudoRequiredSubtitle: Administrator rights are required for this action.
backup.i18n.clone.loadTargets.title: Load target drives
backup.i18n.clone.loadTargets.subtitle: Sudo enables detection of unmounted NVMe/USB drives. Password is saved for cloning.
backup.i18n.settings.save.title: Save backup settings
backup.i18n.settings.save.subtitle: Saves settings and configures the timer.
backup.i18n.schedule.runNow.title: Run scheduled backup now
backup.i18n.schedule.runNow.subtitle: Runs the backup runner immediately.
backup.i18n.cloud.loadFailed: External backups could not be loaded
backup.i18n.cloud.loadFailedOffline: External backups could not be loaded (server unreachable)
backup.i18n.usb.infoLoadFailed: USB info could not be loaded
backup.i18n.sudoSaveFailed: Sudo password could not be saved
backup.i18n.actionTimeout: The action took too long. Check server or try “Save without verification”.
backup.i18n.usb.selectFirst: Please select a USB drive first
backup.i18n.usb.prepare.title: Prepare USB
backup.i18n.usb.prepare.subtitle: Formatting/renaming requires sudo.
backup.i18n.usb.ejectConfirm: Really eject USB? You can safely remove it afterwards.
backup.i18n.usb.eject.title: Eject USB
backup.i18n.usb.eject.subtitle: Unmount + sync (optional power-off).
backup.i18n.usb.mount.title: Mount USB
backup.i18n.usb.mount.subtitle: USB drive will be mounted (non-destructive).
backup.i18n.clone.confirmBody:
Do you want to clone the system to {{target}}?

All data on the target drive will be overwritten. Boot stays on SD card, root filesystem is loaded from target drive (hybrid boot).

Please reboot after cloning (sudo reboot).
backup.i18n.clone.start.title: Clone system
backup.i18n.clone.start.subtitle: Clone requires sudo (mount, rsync).
backup.i18n.usb.notMountedConfirmMount: USB drive is not mounted. Mount it now?
backup.i18n.cloud.uploadDisabled: Cloud upload is disabled. Please enable it in Settings → Cloud backup settings.
backup.i18n.cloud.settingsMissing: Cloud settings missing. Please configure in Settings → Cloud backup settings.
backup.i18n.cloud.s3SettingsMissing: S3 settings missing. Please configure in Settings → Cloud backup settings.
backup.i18n.cloud.gcsSettingsMissing: GCS settings missing. Please configure in Settings → Cloud backup settings.
backup.i18n.cloud.azureSettingsMissing: Azure settings missing. Please configure in Settings → Cloud backup settings.
backup.i18n.cloud.targetCloudOnly: Cloud (local file is removed after successful upload)
backup.i18n.create.confirm:
Do you really want to create a {{type}} backup?

Target: {{target}}
backup.i18n.create.title: Create backup
backup.i18n.create.subtitle: Backup requires sudo (tar/filesystem access).
backup.i18n.delete.single.title: Delete backup
backup.i18n.delete.single.subtitle: Deletes the backup file (with sudo if needed).
backup.i18n.delete.multiple.title: Delete backups
backup.i18n.delete.multiple.subtitle: Deletes {{count}} backup file(s) (with sudo if needed).
backup.i18n.cloud.selectOneForRestore: Please select only one cloud backup for restore
backup.i18n.cloud.restoreUnsupported: Cloud backup restore is not supported yet. Please download the backup manually.
backup.i18n.clone.cancelPending: Clone is being cancelled – please wait.
backup.i18n.cloud.uploadDisabledHint: Cloud upload is disabled. Enable it in settings.
backup.ui.beginner.introBody: Three paths are enough to start: create a backup, check existing archives, or restore carefully when needed. Targets, cloud, and cloning are under “More options” once you’re comfortable with the basics.
backup.ui.beginner.warningRestore: Before restoring: read the warnings – data may be overwritten.
backup.ui.panda.helper: Pick a clear target: default folder, USB stick, or cloud. Before restore, read what will be overwritten – the companion below shows job status.
backup.ui.panda.title: Backup companion
backup.ui.panda.subtitle: The panda represents this area; the traffic light reflects the known release posture (backup core not verified until a real run is proven).
backup.ui.traffic.verified_calm.label: Backup safety
backup.ui.traffic.verified_calm.detail: No open verify/diagnosis warning; restore preview not active.
backup.ui.traffic.verifying_or_unclear.d: Verify active or intermediate state – wait for the result or read the hints.
backup.ui.traffic.diagnosis_or_restore_r: Verify/diagnosis reports an issue or restore preview – data may be affected.
backup.ui.traffic.core_unverified.label: Core not verified
backup.ui.traffic.core_unverified.detail: Some paths are tested; a successful backup run is not proven.
backup.ui.traffic.runtime_blocked_sudo.d: Backup run is currently blocked (sudo required).
backup.ui.trafficReason.yellow: Release posture: partial paths tested; core backup run not proven (Phase 3 assessment).
backup.ui.trafficReason.red: Release posture: critical blocker or elevated risk (e.g. sudo required, or restore preview/diagnosis).
backup.ui.card.createDesc: Create a backup – USB, local folder, or cloud (if configured).
backup.ui.card.verifyDesc: List archives, verify integrity – without restoring yet.
backup.ui.card.restoreDesc: Only if you intend to roll back – read the warnings on the next screen.
backup.ui.usbDialog.title: Prepare USB
backup.ui.usbDialog.warningBody: If you enable “Format”, all data on the stick will be erased permanently.
backup.ui.usbDialog.formatHint: Recommended if the stick is read-only (e.g. ISO/boot stick).
backup.ui.usbDialog.subtitle: Optionally format (erase) and/or rename (label).
backup.ui.usbDialog.warningExtra: Make sure you selected the correct drive.
backup.ui.usbDialog.labelHelp: Used as the volume name. (Allowed: letters, digits, spaces, _ and -)
backup.ui.pageHeader.title: Backup & restore
backup.ui.pageHeader.subtitle: Back up your system and restore data safely when needed – {{system}}.
backup.ui.backupTab.flow: Flow: 1. Pick target, 2. Choose backup type, 3. optionally encrypt and start.
backup.ui.hero.oneClickBody: Create a full system backup to USB, cloud, or a local folder. Your data and settings are protected.
backup.ui.hero.oneClickNote: Pick the target and backup type below (full recommended), then click create backup.
backup.ui.step1.title: Step 1: Choose backup target
backup.ui.cloud.toastChosen: Cloud target: upload when done; local file removed after verification.
backup.ui.cloud.boxBody: Upload via WebDAV (Settings → Cloud). Local file is deleted after successful upload and verification.
backup.ui.cloud.disabledHint: Cloud upload is disabled. Enable it in backup settings.
backup.ui.usb.noDriveBody: No USB stick reported. After eject: unplug and replug, then “Scan again”.
backup.ui.usb.writeTestHint: Note: write test failed. The stick may be read-only or use the wrong filesystem. “Prepare USB…” may help (data loss possible).
backup.ui.customDir.label: Backup directory (absolute path)
backup.ui.targetStatus.title: Target status
backup.ui.step2.title: Step 2: Backup type
backup.ui.backupType.full.label: Full
backup.ui.backupType.full.hint: Full system backup including OS, packages, and files. Best for first backup or major changes.
backup.ui.backupType.incremental.label: Incremental
backup.ui.backupType.incremental.hint: Only changes since the last full backup. Faster and smaller; full backup required to restore.
backup.ui.backupType.data.label: Data
backup.ui.backupType.data.hint: User data only (/home, /var/www, /opt). Fast, but no OS backup.
backup.ui.step3.title: Step 3: Optional encryption
backup.ui.encrypt.hintGpg: GPG: without a key, only compression
backup.ui.notices.title: ⚠️ Important notes
backup.ui.restore.flow: Flow: 1. Pick target & medium, 2. Select a backup in the list, 3. optionally verify, then restore.
backup.ui.restore.listHint: Start with step 1: pick target. Then select a backup and click restore.
backup.ui.local.emptyHint: Tip: check the target directory in the quick switcher above
backup.ui.settings.incrementalPersonal: Incremental (based on last personal full for this rule)
backup.ui.cloudCompanion.helperPartAfter: , then refresh the list. Without cloud enabled or matches, the light stays cautious.
backup.ui.cloudCompanion.title: Cloud companion
backup.ui.cloudCompanion.subtitle: Panda for external storage; the light reflects loading, configuration, and whether backups were found.
backup.ui.clone.introBody: Clones the running root filesystem from the SD card to a target drive (e.g. NVMe, USB SSD). Boot stays on SD; root loads from the target (hybrid boot). Reboot afterwards (sudo reboot).
backup.ui.clone.introNote: Only ext4 targets are listed. All data on the target will be overwritten.
backup.ui.clone.noTargets: No ext4 target drives found (NVMe, USB SSD).
backup.ui.clone.noteBoot: • Boot stays on SD (kernel loaded from there)
backup.ui.clone.noteRoot: • Root filesystem loads from target (hybrid boot)
backup.ui.clone.noteSd: • Do not remove SD – it still holds boot
backup.ui.settings.lead: Backup target, schedules, and advanced options.
backup.ui.settings.title: Backup settings & scheduling
radioPlayer.error.streamFailed: Stream failed. Check: backend running, audio output in Settings → Sound.
radioPlayer.nativeAppHintBefore: Tip: If the page does not load or the window appears on HDMI, use the native app on the Pi: desktop icon “DSI Radio” or
radioPlayer.soundHint: Audio uses the system output device (default: case speakers).
radioPlayer.streamPlaybackError: Could not play stream (e.g. proxy/CORS or codec).
settings.toast.logsLoadFailedServer: Could not load logs. Server unreachable.
settings.toast.saveFailedRemote: Save failed. Server unreachable – start the server and try again.
settings.sudo.cloudSave.title: Save cloud settings
settings.sudo.cloudSave.subtitle: Saves cloud configuration for backups.
settings.toast.webdavFill: Please fill in WebDAV URL, username, and password
settings.confirm.reboot: Really restart the system? Save all changes first.
settings.sudo.reboot.title: Restart system
settings.sudo.reboot.subtitle: The system will restart. The app will be briefly unavailable.
settings.sudoModal.title: Sudo password required
settings.sudoModal.subtitle: Administrator rights are required for this action.
mailServer.toast.notAvailable: This feature is not available yet. The backend endpoint is still being implemented.
mailServer.intro: A mail server lets you send and receive email from your system.
mailServer.config.title: Configuration
mailServer.components.title: 📧 Mail server components
mailServer.component.postfix.title: Postfix (SMTP)
mailServer.component.dovecot.title: Dovecot (IMAP/POP3)
mailServer.component.spam.title: SpamAssassin
mailServer.dns.title: Important DNS records required
mailServer.tls.title: 🔒 TLS/SSL certificates
mailServer.tls.body: After installation, free Let's Encrypt certificates are installed.
mailServer.disabled.hint: Enable the mail server above to install it.
mailServer.panel.hint.title: ℹ️ Note
mailServer.panel.hint.li3: • Needs a domain with MX records
mailServer.panel.req.title: ⚠️ Requirements
mailServer.panel.next.title: 📚 Next steps
mailServer.panel.ports.title: 🔐 Ports
documentation.sidebar.heading: Documentation
documentation.intro.lead: Use the chapters on the left for guides, screenshots, and step-by-step instructions for each area. The knowledge base also covers the FAQ, experience level (beginner/advanced), panda companions, and guided flows — see the Dashboard chapter and docs/user/GUIDED_UX_AND_COMPANION.md.
documentation.bodyLanguageNote: The detailed text in each chapter below is still in German. Many topics are available in English on the website (setuphelfer.de — documentation, tutorials, troubleshooting). Full in-app translation of this manual is in progress.
documentation.nav.dualdisplay: Dual display DSI0 + HDMI1 – two monitors
musicbox.page.title: Music box
musicbox.page.subtitle: Music box – {{pageSubtitleLabel}}
musicbox.panda.strip: Pick your music server and start installation if needed. If Mopidy only shows plain text, Iris is often missing—run “Start installation” again. Status and diagnostics are further down the page.
musicbox.panda.companionSubtitle: The panda guides music setup; the traffic light shows whether the selected service is installed and active.
musicbox.diagnose.title: Mopidy / Iris diagnostics
musicbox.diagnose.intro: If Iris does not load at http://localhost:6680/iris: run diagnostics (once “Start installation” with sudo password allows deeper checks).
musicbox.diagnose.irisMopidyNoDetail: ✗ No (→ install Iris for user mopidy)
musicbox.diagnose.sudoHint: Sudo not set—for full diagnostics run “Start installation” once with sudo password.
musicbox.status.title: Status
musicbox.serverSelect.title: Choose music server
musicbox.mixer.manualHint: Installation failed. Run manually in a terminal:
musicbox.sudo.installTitle: Sudo password for music box installation
musicbox.sudo.installSubtitle: Administrator rights are required to install Mopidy, internet radio, AirPlay, etc.
musicbox.sudo.mixerSubtitle: pavucontrol and qpwgraph are installed via apt; administrator rights are required.
